summary: Matched Captain Falcon `ftCa_SpecialHi` by deleting its 848-line assembly file and switching the build to compile `src/melee/ft/chara/ftCaptain/ftCa_SpecialHi.c` as Matching. The PR also made partial progress on `ftCa_SpecialLw`, including implementing/exporting `ftCa_SpecialHi_800E3EAC`, adding inline helpers for repeated flag/friction logic, and documenting that `ftCa_SpecialAirLwEnd_Phys` remained non-matching.
what_changed: Deleted `asm/melee/ft/chara/ftCaptain/ftCa_SpecialHi.s`, removing the assembly fallback for the SpecialHi file.
Changed `configure.py` so `melee/ft/chara/ftCaptain/ftCa_SpecialHi.c` is marked `Object(Matching, ...)` instead of `Object(NonMatching, ...)`.
Simplified `ftCa_SpecialAirHi_Coll` into a direct call to `ftCa_SpecialHi_Coll`.
Adjusted `ftCa_SpecialHi_Phys` to use `ca->air_drift_max` from `ftCo_DatAttrs* ca = &fp->co_attrs` in the `ftCommon_8007D050` call.
Changed `ftCa_SpecialHiThrow0_Phys` to use `PAD_STACK(32)`, call `ftCa_SpecialHi_Phys(gobj)` when `fp->mv.ca.specialhi.x2_b0` is set, then continue with catch gravity logic.
Removed local literal definitions from `ftCa_SpecialHi.c` for `ftCa_SpecialLw_804D9230`, `ftCa_SpecialLw_804D9234`, and `ftCa_SpecialLw_804D9238`.

Implemented `ftCa_SpecialHi_800E3EAC`, including throw flag consumption, foot joint selection, Captain/Ganon-specific effect spawning through `efAsync_Spawn`, and a fallback call to `ftCommon_8007DB24`.
Added small inline helpers in `ftCa_SpecialLw.c` for throw flag checking, command flag clearing, animation transition setup, air animation flag reset, and multiplying velocity by special-lw friction.
Changed several numeric constants to explicit float literals such as `0.0F` and `1.0F` in SpecialLw setup and calls.

Adjusted `ftCa_SpecialLw_Coll` matching shape by removing a temporary `facing_dir` variable, using direct `fp->facing_dir` comparisons, and factoring command-variable reset into `ftCa_Special_Inline_SetFlags`.
smart_moves: Reused already-matching shared behavior: `ftCa_SpecialAirHi_Phys` and `ftCa_SpecialAirHi_Coll` became wrappers around the grounded SpecialHi physics/collision routines, reducing duplicate code while preserving stack shape with `PAD_STACK`.

Introduced narrow `static inline` helpers for repeated flag reset and friction code in `ftCa_SpecialLw.c`, a common decomp tactic for getting repeated emitted instruction sequences without spreading copy-paste everywhere.
Used `PAD_STACK(24/28/32)` where the source body was logically smaller than the original stack frame, preserving codegen while keeping the C readable.
Made matching-sensitive type/literal choices explicit: examples include `0.0F`, `1.0F`, and passing `0.0F` to `ftParts_80075CB4`.
Kept partial work honest by marking only `ftCa_SpecialHi.c` as Matching while leaving `ftCa_SpecialLw.c` NonMatching and noting `ftCa_SpecialAirLwEnd_Phys` as still non-matching.
Added the newly externalized `ftCa_SpecialHi_800E3EAC` prototype to the SpecialLw header rather than relying on a file-local static declaration.
decomp_lessons: When a ground/air variant compiles to the same behavior, try replacing duplicate bodies with a small wrapper plus `PAD_STACK`; this PR did that for SpecialAirHi physics and collision.

For fighter move code, matching can depend on using the right common attribute pointer: the SpecialHi physics hunk uses `ftCo_DatAttrs* ca = &fp->co_attrs` and `ca->air_drift_max` rather than a different field access shape.
Bitfield identity matters. `ftCa_SpecialHiThrow0_Phys` changed the condition from `fp->mv.ca.specialhi.x2_b2` to `x2_b0`, indicating that matching or correctness may hinge on the exact named bit.
Small inline functions can be useful for repeated command-variable and throw-flag resets because they may preserve a desired code pattern while keeping code understandable.
Effect-spawn routines may need character-kind branching even inside a Captain file: `ftCa_SpecialHi_800E3EAC` checks `FTKIND_CAPTAIN` versus `FTKIND_GANON` and uses different effect IDs.
Do not flip build metadata to Matching until the corresponding asm object is removed from `obj_files.mk`; this PR updated `configure.py`, `obj_files.mk`, and deleted the asm file together.
The PR body explicitly says `ftCa_SpecialAirLwEnd_Phys` remained non-matching, so future work should not assume all of `ftCa_SpecialLw.c` matched.
naming_conventions: `ftCa_` is used for Captain Falcon fighter functions.
Special move files follow `ftCa_SpecialHi.c`, `ftCa_SpecialLw.c`, and matching headers such as `ftCa_SpecialLw.h`.
Lifecycle callbacks use suffixes such as `_Enter`, `_Anim`, `_IASA`, `_Phys`, and `_Coll`.
Air variants are named with `SpecialAir...`, for example `ftCa_SpecialAirHi_Phys` and `ftCa_SpecialAirHi_Coll`.
Unknown/address-based helpers retain address-style names such as `ftCa_SpecialHi_800E3EAC`.
Header prototypes include address comments, shown by `/* 0E3EAC */ void ftCa_SpecialHi_800E3EAC(HSD_GObj* gobj);`.
Local matching helpers in this PR used names like `ftCa_Special_Inline_Check_Flag`, `ftCa_Special_Inline_SetFlags`, and `ftCa_Special_Inline_Friction`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Deleted the full `ftCa_SpecialHi.s` assembly file once the C matched.
Switched build selection from asm object to C object in `obj_files.mk` and from `NonMatching` to `Matching` in `configure.py`.
Used `PAD_STACK(28)`, `PAD_STACK(32)`, and `PAD_STACK(24)` to recover stack-frame sizes after simplifying C bodies.
Collapsed duplicated air SpecialHi bodies into calls to matching ground SpecialHi functions to match branch/call structure.
Used inline helpers for repeated code sequences instead of macros or duplicated open-coded blocks.
Adjusted temporary variable usage in `ftCa_SpecialLw_Coll`; removing the `facing_dir` local changed the expression shape to direct `fp->facing_dir` comparisons.
Used explicit float constants and float-typed zero arguments to influence generated floating-point code.
Kept `ftCa_SpecialLw.c` as NonMatching because the PR body identified `ftCa_SpecialAirLwEnd_Phys` as still unresolved.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in this PR slice.
The only human note in the slice was the PR body statement: `Non-matching funcs: ftCa_SpecialAirLwEnd_Phys in ftCa_SpecialLw`.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that match `ftCa_SpecialAirLwEnd_Phys` in `ftCa_SpecialLw.c`.
Search for later renames or documentation of `ftCa_SpecialHi_800E3EAC` and its effect-spawn behavior.
Compare other fighter special move PRs that use wrapper functions plus `PAD_STACK` to match ground/air variants.
Look for follow-up struct/bitfield work around `fp->mv.ca.specialhi.x2_b0` and `x2_b2`.
